🎯 How to Use This Tool

Interactive Visual Guide · 5 Steps

1 The Big Picture

A Guided Carousel → Results Below

The tool has two zones. At the top, six selection panels are presented in a carousel that shows two panels at a time. Use the ‹ › arrows, the clickable step pills, or swipe on mobile to navigate three pages.

Below the carousel, personalized AI tools and activities appear instantly. A chip bar between the zones shows your current selections at a glance.

You only need two selections to start — the more you add, the more precise the matches.

Step Indicator Pills (clickable)

💡 Tip: The step pills at the top track your progress — completed steps light up, and the current page pair is highlighted. Click any pill to jump to that page.
2 Select Your Context

Navigate the Carousel, Two Panels at a Time

The six selection panels are spread across three carousel pages:

Page 1: Faculty Focus + Time & Depth
Page 2: Audience & Modality + Discipline
Page 3: Teaching Task + Bloom's Level & Topic

Click any option to select it. Once both panels on a page have a selection, the carousel auto-advances to the next page.

Page 1 — Both selected → auto-advance

💡 Tip: You can also navigate with arrow keys (← →), swipe on touchscreens, or click the step pills at the top to jump to any page directly.
3 Refine Your Search

Add a Keyword or Bloom's Level

On page 3 of the carousel, the Bloom's Level & Topic panel lets you optionally type a keyword or topic to search across all 500 activities — titles, descriptions, tags, and full instructions.

Filter by Bloom's taxonomy level to match the cognitive complexity you want. The matching engine now checks Bloom's at both the rule level and individual activity level for precise results.

💡 Tip: Try keywords like "ethics," "rubric," "simulation," "data," "LibGuide," or "PICO" to find matches for your topic.
4 Explore Results

Tools, Activities & Category Badges

Results appear below the carousel. Recommended AI Tools shows top-ranked tools with links, badges, and descriptions.

Suggested Activities shows assignments with time estimates, Bloom's levels, and a category badge (e.g., "Library & Info Science," "Health Sciences & Nursing") so you can immediately see the discipline context.

Click any activity card to expand full step-by-step implementation instructions with source provenance.

💡 Tip: Instructions include tool recommendations, prompts, workflows, and linked source citations you can implement the same day.
5 Share & Explore

Save, Share, or Start Fresh

Click 📋 Copy Link to save a permanent URL of your selections. Share with colleagues or bookmark it.

Click ✕ Reset All to clear everything and return to page 1 of the carousel, or ↻ Regenerate to refresh with the same selections.

Click any tool name to visit its website directly.
